rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Paul Ryan Associates For Violation Of Judge Miller'S July 1, 2013 Order Compelling Production Of Documents Or Alternatively For Issue Evidence And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 14, 2013, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO.'s Amended Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Paul Ryan Associates For Violation Of Judge Miller's July 1, 2013 Order Compelling Production Of Documents Or Alternatively For Issue Evidence And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court requests that at the hearing the moving party identify precisely the items that Paul Ryan Associates has not produced in conformity with Judge Miller's order. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA L. PETERSON VS. US BANCORP DBA US BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Paul Motion, A U.S. Bank National Association Employee'S Motion To Quash Service Of Process
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2013, Line 3: Defendant Paul Motion, A U.S. Bank National Association Employee's Motion To Quash Service Of Process Granted. (CCP § 418.10.) Plaintiff has not met her initial burden of proof to demonstrate that minimum contacts exist between Mr. Morton and California. (Mihlon v. Sup.Ct. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) Neither the Complaint nor Plaintiff's opposition addresses the issue of minimum contacts. Furthermore, Plaintiff has not met her burden of proof to show the facts requisite to an effective service. (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413.) Plaintiff's reliance on a purported statement made by Mr. Foote at a court hearing held on September 13, 2011, which predates the doe substitution of Mr. Morton on August 22, 2012 by approximately 11 months, is insufficient to demonstrate effective service. (Peterson Decl., 1.) Further, Mr. Foote states that he is not authorized to accept service on behalf of Mr. Morton . (Foote Decl., 3.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: DAWN-SHEMAIN WEEKS et al VS. CMEA DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2013, Line 9: Plaintiffs DAWN-SHEMAIN WEEKS', MARGARET HINES', and SHANNON SCHLAGENHAUF'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery The Court adopts the reasoning and analysis in Pro Tem Judge Rosenquist's report in its entirety. The motion to compel is granted, subject to the terms of the parties' previously stipulated protective order designating the Haag separation agreement and documents and the Handelsman separation agreement as "Attorney Eyes Only" - restricting disclosure of the documents initially to counsel for the parties and excluding the parties themselves unless subsequently ordered or permitted by the court. Defendants may redact payment information pertaining solely to amounts paid to Haag and Handelsman to buy their equity or to the identity of other equity owners of CMEA. Defendants may not redact payment information made to Haag or Handelsman with respect to any payment made for defense costs or indemnity of Haag or Handelsman with respect to the case at bar. If defendants make other redactions in the documents, each such redaction must be supported by a privilege log specifically describing the redaction, the grounds for the redaction, and providing sufficient factual information for the court to rule upon such a redaction if requested to do so. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN GRIFFITHS VS. AON CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2013, Line 12: Cross Defendant JONATHAN GRIFFITHS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled. As to the eighth cause of action, Defendant states a cause of action for conversion. Plaintiff cannot make a demurrer to part of a cause of action. Defendant's allegation that plaintiff submitted false expense reports pleads a specific sum capable of identification. (First Amended Cross Complaint "FACC", 81. The Court overrules the demurrers to the remaining causes of action (2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th). The California Uniform Trade Secrets Act ("CUTSA") does not preempt those causes of action because those claims allege a broader spectrum of misconduct that is not based on the same nucleus of facts as the misappropriation cause of action. The Court denies the motion to strike. Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1322(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN GRIFFITHS VS. AON CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2013, Line 12: Plaintiff JONATHAN GRIFFITHS' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. First, the motion seeks a ruling regarding whether paragraph 8(a)(iii) of the parties' contract is enforceable, but the Complaint does not clearly refer to that provision. As a separate basis for denying the motion, CCP 438(c)(1)(A) provides that the Court can grant judgment on the pleadings in favor of the plaintiff: If the moving party is a plaintiff, that the complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause or causes of action against the defendant and the answer does not state facts sufficient to constitute a defense to the complaint. (emphasis added). In this case, defendant's answer denies all of plaintiff's allegations and plaintiff fails to address defendant's defenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR S. BRICE VS. MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Matiasic Roth & Johnson Llp'S And Paul Matiasic'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 14, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTS MATIASIC ROTH & JOHNSON LLP AND PAUL MATIASIC'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted pending resolution of the complaint and the filing of an answer. Responses to discovery will be due thirty (30) days from the filing of an answer to the amended complaint. The reason for granting the motion is that the discovery is voluminous and primarily consists of contention interrogatories and RFA's. Until such time as the initial pleadings have been determined, contention intoerrogatories and related matters are premature. Responding to the multiple discovery sets prior to resolution of the complaint is unduly burdensome on responding party. Request for sanctions is denied. Objection to the filing of the Reply is overruled and leave for late filing granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ONE BLOCK OFF THE GRID INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. JEFF RICHARDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Joinder In Deft Jeff Richards Mtn For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2013, Line 16: Defendant DAMIAN SCHWARTZ'S Motion For Joinder In Defendant Jeff Richards' Motion For Change Of Venue. The ruling issued on Defendant Richards' motion will also apply to Defendant Schwartz. The tentative ruling is as follows: Denied. Defendants have not met their burden of establishing that there is no statutory basis for venue in San Francisco. (Fontaine v. Sup. Ct. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 830, 836.) The complaint alleges breach of three specific contracts. (Complaint, Exhibits A - C.) Defendants have not provided sufficient evidence to establish that these specific contracts were not entered into in San Francisco. The Declaration of Jeff Richards states at 5 that he faxed documents to Plaintiff, but does not establish that the faxed documents were the specific contracts forming the basis of the Complaint. Thus, venue is proper in San Francisco under CCP § 395(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
